# UC-22.21.17 · Technical Documentation Completeness Checklist (Art. 11/Annex IV)

## Description

Scores required documentation sections present per model_id for Annex IV technical documentation completeness.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_docs and sourcetypes ai:tdoc:status. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Define canonical section_id list; (2) integrate CMS webhook; (3) block EU deploy if completeness < 100; (4) store PDF hashes externally. Indexes required: `index=ai_docs`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:tdoc:status`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_docs` HEC — model_id, section_id, present.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define canonical section_id list; (2) integrate CMS webhook; (3) block EU deploy if completeness < 100; (4) store PDF hashes externally.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_docs sourcetype="ai:tdoc:status" earliest=-7d
| stats values(section_id) as sections, sum(present) as present_cnt by model_id
| eval required=12
| eval completeness=round(100*present_cnt/required,2)
| where completeness < 100
| sort + completeness
```

Understanding this SPL

**Technical Documentation Completeness Checklist (Art. 11/Annex IV) (EU AI Act)** — Scores required documentation sections present per model_id for Annex IV technical documentation completeness.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_docs` HEC — model_id, section_id, present. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_docs; **sourcetype**: ai:tdoc:status. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_docs, sourcetype="ai:tdoc:status", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by model_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **required** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **completeness** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where completeness < 100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge-style single value (avg completeness), Table (missing sections), Bar chart (by model_id)

## SPL

```spl
index=ai_docs sourcetype="ai:tdoc:status" earliest=-7d
| stats values(section_id) as sections, sum(present) as present_cnt by model_id
| eval required=12
| eval completeness=round(100*present_cnt/required,2)
| where completeness < 100
| sort + completeness
```

## Visualization

Gauge-style single value (avg completeness), Table (missing sections), Bar chart (by model_id)
